mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, supporting equity multiples and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior month, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent trend implies hotter inflation and a higher expected policy path, which compresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation breakout episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the return relationship flips sign for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS growth identifies a weakening labor market that will soon reduce earnings expectations and corporate risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor deterioration episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns on average, or if the joint signal adds no explanatory power beyond UNRATE alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumption and revenue growth, which supports near-term equity earnings surprises and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS momentum does not precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than chance, or if the 1-year rolling hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in INDPRO after a prior slowdown signals improving industrial output and cyclical earnings leverage, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if industrial production rebounds are followed by flat or negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in most cases, or if the signal loses significance after a rolling 3-year evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises while RSAFS also rises, the economy is showing synchronized housing and consumption demand strength, which should transmit into positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise above their 3-month averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-demand condition does not outperform either proxy alone in predicting 60-day returns, or if triggered episodes do not have a positive average return.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE during an expansionary phase supports household income growth and reduces recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment periods fail to produce above-baseline 20-day returns, or if the sign becomes negative during two or more consecutive expansionary subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with CPIAUCSL above trend compresses valuation multiples and raises real-rate pressure, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 2.5% and CPIAUCSL is accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-inflation episodes do not lead to negative 5-day returns more often than not, or if the interaction term has no incremental predictive value versus either series alone.
